text: Which part of the foot is commonly used for a controlled short pass in football?
correct_answer: Inside of the foot

text: What is an own goal in football?
options: A goal scored into a team's own net
A goal scored by the captain only
A goal from a free kick only
A goal scored after extra time
correct_answer: A goal scored into a team's own net

text: What is a clean sheet in football?
correct_answer: A team concedes no goals

text: Which official usually runs along the touchline to help judge offside and throw-ins?
correct_answer: Assistant referee

text: What is the center circle used for at kick-off in football?
options: Keeping opponents a set distance from the ball
correct_answer: Keeping opponents a set distance from the ball

text: What is a through ball in football?
options: A pass played into space behind defenders
A shot directly from a corner
correct_answer: A pass played into space behind defenders

text: What is pressing in football tactics?
options: Applying pressure to win the ball back
Holding the ball near the corner flag
correct_answer: Applying pressure to win the ball back

text: What is the penalty arc for?
options: Keeping players outside ten yards from the penalty spot
correct_answer: Keeping players outside ten yards from the penalty spot

text: What is stoppage time in football?
options: Time added for delays during a half
A mandatory timeout after every goal
correct_answer: Time added for delays during a half

text: Which restart is awarded when an attacker last touches the ball before it crosses the defending team's goal line without a goal?
correct_answer: Goal kick

text: Which restart is awarded when a defender last touches the ball before it crosses their own goal line without a goal?
correct_answer: Corner kick

text: What is a one-two pass in football?
options: A quick pass and return between two teammates
A shot that hits both posts
correct_answer: A quick pass and return between two teammates

text: What is a layup in basketball?
options: A close-range shot taken near the basket
A shot from behind the backboard
correct_answer: A close-range shot taken near the basket

text: What is a dunk in basketball?
options: Scoring by forcing the ball downward through the hoop
Passing the ball between two defenders
Bouncing the ball off the floor twice
correct_answer: Scoring by forcing the ball downward through the hoop

text: What is a steal in basketball?
options: Taking possession from the opponent legally
Standing in the paint too long
correct_answer: Taking possession from the opponent legally

text: What is a block in basketball?
options: Deflecting or stopping an opponent's shot attempt
Throwing the ball from out of bounds
correct_answer: Deflecting or stopping an opponent's shot attempt

text: What is a pick-and-roll?
options: A screen followed by a move toward the basket
A jump ball at center court
correct_answer: A screen followed by a move toward the basket

text: What does boxing out mean in basketball?
options: Positioning the body to secure a rebound
Leaving the court after a foul
correct_answer: Positioning the body to secure a rebound

text: What is a bounce pass?
options: A pass that hits the floor before reaching a teammate
A pass thrown into the crowd
A shot that bounces off the rim
correct_answer: A pass that hits the floor before reaching a teammate

text: What is a jump ball used for?
options: Starting play or resolving certain possession situations
correct_answer: Starting play or resolving certain possession situations

text: What is a charge in basketball?
options: An offensive foul for illegal contact into a defender
A legal block by the rim
correct_answer: An offensive foul for illegal contact into a defender

text: What is a zone defense?
options: Defenders guard areas instead of specific players
All players guard the ball handler only
correct_answer: Defenders guard areas instead of specific players

text: What is a buzzer-beater?
options: A shot made just before the game clock expires
A missed layup after a timeout
correct_answer: A shot made just before the game clock expires

text: What is the baseline in basketball?
options: The end line behind each basket
correct_answer: The end line behind each basket

text: What is a drop shot in tennis?
options: A soft shot intended to land just over the net
A serve hit at maximum speed
A shot hit after the ball bounces twice
A ball thrown by the umpire
correct_answer: A soft shot intended to land just over the net

text: What is a slice in tennis?
options: A shot hit with backspin or sidespin
A shot that must land outside the court
A serve with no spin allowed
correct_answer: A shot hit with backspin or sidespin

text: What is a smash in tennis?
options: A soft return near the net
correct_answer: A powerful overhead shot

text: What is advantage in tennis?
options: The point after deuce won by one player
A serve that touches the net
correct_answer: The point after deuce won by one player

text: What is a let serve in tennis?
options: A serve that touches the net and lands in the correct service box
A serve that lands outside the court
A serve after a match ends
A serve hit with the wrong racket
correct_answer: A serve that touches the net and lands in the correct service box

text: Which shot starts each point in tennis?
correct_answer: Serve

text: What is a baseline rally?
options: An exchange of shots mostly from the back of the court
A coin toss before the match
A series of net cords only
correct_answer: An exchange of shots mostly from the back of the court

text: What is a break point?
options: A point where the receiver can win the server's game
A point after a racket breaks
correct_answer: A point where the receiver can win the server's game

text: What is a foot fault in tennis?
options: An illegal foot movement during the serve
A ball bouncing on the baseline
A player slipping after the point
correct_answer: An illegal foot movement during the serve

text: What is the service box in tennis?
options: The area where a serve must land
correct_answer: The area where a serve must land

text: What is a forehand in tennis?
options: A stroke hit on the dominant-hand side of the body
A serve hit below the waist
correct_answer: A stroke hit on the dominant-hand side of the body

text: What is a backhand in tennis?
options: A stroke hit across the body from the non-dominant side
A ball change after a set
correct_answer: A stroke hit across the body from the non-dominant side

text: What does a green flag usually signal in circuit racing?
correct_answer: Racing may proceed

text: What does a blue flag usually tell a slower driver?
options: A faster car is approaching to overtake
correct_answer: A faster car is approaching to overtake

text: What is a formation lap?
options: A lap before the race start to prepare cars and grid
correct_answer: A lap before the race start to prepare cars and grid

text: What is a safety car period?
options: A controlled period where cars slow and follow a safety car
A time when only mechanics drive
correct_answer: A controlled period where cars slow and follow a safety car

text: What is a racing apex?
options: The inside point of a corner a driver aims for
correct_answer: The inside point of a corner a driver aims for

text: What is tire degradation in racing?
options: Loss of tire performance through wear or heat
correct_answer: Loss of tire performance through wear or heat

text: What is a racing line?
options: The path around a circuit that aims to be fastest
The line of spectators at a gate
A painted logo on a helmet
correct_answer: The path around a circuit that aims to be fastest

text: What is a hairpin corner?
correct_answer: A very tight turn

text: What does grid position refer to?
options: A driver's starting place for a race
correct_answer: A driver's starting place for a race

text: What is aerodynamic drag?
options: Air resistance acting against a moving car
correct_answer: Air resistance acting against a moving car

text: What is a gravel trap used for on a circuit?
options: Helping slow cars that leave the track
correct_answer: Helping slow cars that leave the track

text: What is parc ferme in many motorsport series?
options: A restricted area and rule state limiting car changes
correct_answer: A restricted area and rule state limiting car changes

text: What is a clinch in combat sports?
correct_answer: Close-range grappling or holding position

text: What is a submission in grappling or MMA?
options: Forcing an opponent to concede through a hold
correct_answer: Forcing an opponent to concede through a hold

text: What is a rear-naked choke?
options: A choke applied from behind without using the uniform collar
correct_answer: A choke applied from behind without using the uniform collar

text: What is a guard position in grappling?
options: A ground position where one fighter controls from their back or legs
correct_answer: A ground position where one fighter controls from their back or legs

text: What is a round in combat sports?
options: A timed segment of a bout
correct_answer: A timed segment of a bout

text: What is a mouthguard used for?
correct_answer: Protecting the teeth and mouth

text: What is shadowboxing?
correct_answer: Practicing strikes without an opponent

text: What is a sprawl in wrestling or MMA?
options: A defensive movement to counter a takedown
correct_answer: A defensive movement to counter a takedown

text: What does unanimous decision mean in combat sports?
options: All judges score the same fighter as winner
The referee stops the fight instantly
correct_answer: All judges score the same fighter as winner

text: What is a knockdown in boxing?
options: A fighter is sent to the canvas or held up by ropes after a legal blow
A glove falls off before the bell
correct_answer: A fighter is sent to the canvas or held up by ropes after a legal blow

text: What is hand wrapping used for in boxing and MMA?
options: Supporting and protecting the hands and wrists
correct_answer: Supporting and protecting the hands and wrists

text: What is a split decision?
options: Judges are divided, but one fighter wins on more scorecards
The bout is split into two arenas
A fight with no judges present
correct_answer: Judges are divided, but one fighter wins on more scorecards

text: What is a respawn in many video games?
options: A defeated player returning to play
correct_answer: A defeated player returning to play

text: What is a minimap in many competitive games?
options: A small map display showing key game information
correct_answer: A small map display showing key game information

text: What does AoE commonly mean in games?
correct_answer: Area of Effect

text: What is cooldown in game mechanics?
options: A waiting period before an ability can be used again
correct_answer: A waiting period before an ability can be used again

text: What is a bracket in esports tournaments?
options: The structure showing matchups and advancement
correct_answer: The structure showing matchups and advancement

text: What is seeding in a tournament?
options: Placing players or teams into a bracket based on ranking or criteria
correct_answer: Placing players or teams into a bracket based on ranking or criteria

text: What is a clutch play?
options: A high-pressure play that helps secure a round or match
correct_answer: A high-pressure play that helps secure a round or match

text: What does PvP stand for?
correct_answer: Player versus Player

text: What does PvE stand for?
correct_answer: Player versus Environment

text: What is a coach's main role in esports?
correct_answer: Helping strategy, preparation, and review

text: What is a replay review used for by esports teams?
options: Studying past gameplay to improve decisions
Changing the official final score automatically
correct_answer: Studying past gameplay to improve decisions

text: What is a support role in many team games?
options: A role focused on enabling or protecting teammates
A role that never joins fights
A role assigned only to spectators
A role that controls tournament audio
correct_answer: A role focused on enabling or protecting teammates
